element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Basic combat capability; replaced with flamethrower on first life loss
Primary offensive tool; replaced by machine gun again upon subsequent deaths
Can destroy rocks to reveal hidden weapon powerups; area-effect capability
Destroys obstacles (rocks) to access hidden upgrades; more powerful than grenades
Damage mitigation; allows limited hits without time expiration (unlike predecessor)
NES-exclusive feature; collected to purchase improved weaponry and armor in shops
Resource management; players spend collected zeny for upgrades
Mini-stage entry points; trigger boss encounters when entered
Major challenge spikes; feature sampled voiceovers; progression gating

typical_relief_triggers: Defeating boss and exiting green door
Finding hidden weapon caches under rocks
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical tension structure with exploration/shopping relief phases interspersed between combat encounters and scripted boss confrontations. Game reuses single-stage design with mini-stage boss arenas, creating repetitive but escalating difficulty rhythm.

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Rocks and obstacles that can be destroyed to reveal hidden weapon powerups; encourages exploration
spatial_grammar.secret_convention: Destroyable rocks (via bazooka or grenades) conceal upgraded weaponry; exploration rewards hidden upgrades

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory combat with basic machine gun and grenade mechanics; shops provide early resource management learning
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon upgrade availability increases; player must manage zeny and choose between armor vs. weaponry purchases; mini-boss encounters introduce through green doors
notable_spikes: Green door entry (boss encounter scripted spike)
Armor system removal on death (vulnerability increases)

notable_level_patterns.description: Game reuses single main stage structure (carried over from predecessor) but intersperses mini-stages accessed via green doors where boss encounters occur. Creates compartmentalized difficulty spikes within continuous exploration.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic stage with scattered green door portals

notable_level_patterns.description: Destroyable rocks and obstacles throughout levels conceal weapon powerups. Players must use grenades or bazooka to access hidden upgrades, rewarding thorough exploration.

NES-exclusive currency system creates resource management gates. Players collect zeny during exploration to purchase armor and weapon improvements at shop locations, creating intentional pacing interruptions.

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Ikari Warriors II: Victory Road (NES) article
